sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me understand the structure better. I see that clicking the top red block shifted blocks in columns 23 and 35 (vertical columns) as well as the top frame's horizontal blocks. This suggests the loops are interconnected.
Let me think about this differently. The structure seems to have:
1. Three horizontal "frames" at rows 17, 26, and 35
2. Two vertical "columns" at cols 23 and 35 that connect the frames
3. Each frame has a red (left) and green (right) block
When I click a red/green block, it shifts the loop associated with that frame. The loops share the vertical column blocks.
Let me try to figure out the loop topology. Looking at the initial state (before my click):
- Top frame: S(17,23), b(17,26), p(17,29), p(17,35)
- Middle frame: w(26,17), b(26,20), b(26,23), w(26,26), S(26,29), p(26,32), g(26,35), S(26,38), g(26,41), g(26,44)
- Bottom frame: w(35,17), S(35,20), b(35,23), w(35,26), b(35,29), S(35,32), p(35,35), b(35,38), g(35,41), S(35,44)
Vertical column 23: S(17,23), p(20,23), g(23,23), b(26,23), w(29,23), b(35,23), g(41,23), S(44,23)
Vertical column 35: p(17,35), b(20,35), S(23,35), g(26,35), w(29,35), g(32,35), p(35,35), w(38,35), b(41,35), g(44,35)
Wait, I need to be more careful. The vertical columns and horizontal frames share blocks. Let me think about this as a graph where blocks are nodes and adjacency defines the loop.
Actually, let me try a simpler approach. Let me click the green block of the top frame to reverse the shift, and then try clicking the middle frame's red block to see its effect.
But first, let me check what the yellow markers indicate. They're at columns 34 and 37, rows 25, 28, 34, 37. These are near the middle and bottom frames. The markers seem to highlight the blocks at columns 32-33 and 35-36 in those areas.
Actually, looking at the image, the yellow markers form a rectangular outline around certain blocks. In the middle frame, they're around the blocks at cols 32 and 35. In the bottom frame, they're also around cols 32 and 35.
Wait, I need to reconsider. The yellow 1x1 blocks are at:
- (25, 34), (25, 37) - above middle frame
- (28, 34), (28, 37) - below middle frame
- (34, 34), (34, 37) - above bottom frame
- (37, 34), (37, 37) - below bottom frame
These form 2x2 rectangular markers around the blocks at (26, 35) and (35, 35). The markers at col 34 are to the left of the block at col 35, and markers at col 37 are to the right of the block at col 35 (or left of block at col 38).
Actually, the block at col 35 spans cols 35-36. So:
- Col 34 is just to the left of this block
- Col 37 is just to the right of this block (or at the left edge of the block at col 38)
Hmm, the yellow markers seem to highlight the blocks at column 35 in the middle and bottom frames. Maybe the goal is to have specific colors at those positions.
Let me try a different approach. Let me click the green block to reverse the shift, then systematically explore the middle and bottom frames.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Let me reverse the shift first, then explore the middle frame.
